intro: Malaxis sordida, commonly known as the sordid malaxis, is a species of orchid native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Malaxis sordida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
